title: Theopsis microphylla
intro: Theopsis microphylla, also known as Epilobium parviflorum, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including urinary tract infections, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Theopsis microphylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Theopsis microphylla is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat urinary issues, fever, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Theopsis microphylla is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It belongs to the genus Theopsis, which is a group of flowering plants.
